title: Hieracium longilobum
intro: Hieracium longilobum, also known as Long-lobed Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its bright yellow flowers and long, narrow leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium longilobum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Hieracium longilobum is classified in the family Asteraceae. This species belongs to the genus Hieracium. It is further grouped into the subgenus Pilosella and the section Hieracium section Hieracium. The species is native to Europe and parts of Asia, but its exact classification may vary depending on the source. More research would be needed to confirm its exact classification.
